title: The Wonderful Wizard of Oz
subtitle: A girl, a road of yellow brick, and three unlikely friends.

s01_kansas.speaker: narrator
s01_kansas.narration: The sky over the prairie turns a strange, sickly green. Aunt Em calls from the cellar door, "Dear! The cyclone — get down here!" But Toto is somewhere in the long grass, barking and barking.

branches.label: Run into the grass to find Toto
branches.outcome: You dashed into the tall, swaying prairie grass, following the sound of the frantic barking.

s02_toto_rescue.speaker: narrator
s02_toto_rescue.narration: You scoop the little black dog into your arms just as the wind lifts everything — the fence, the wagon, the whole house — up into the spinning sky. Toto licks your cheek as Kansas falls away beneath you.

branches.label: Let’s quickly find a place to hide
branches.outcome: You clutched Toto to your chest and raced back toward the farmhouse as the wind began to howl.

s02c_tornado.speaker: narrator
s02c_tornado.narration: The wind howls and the world spins! The house lifts off the prairie and tumbles through the cyclone. Shingles, fence rails, even a startled crow whirl past the window. Everything blurs green and grey — then, with a gentle thump, the spinning stops.

branches.label: Hold on tight!
branches.outcome: You clung to Toto with all your might as the farmhouse spiraled into the heart of the great, grey storm.

s03_munchkinland.speaker: narrator
s03_munchkinland.narration: All at once, small cheerful people appear from behind the flowers — popping up from every direction! A beautiful witch in pink smiles. "You are a hero! Your house has crushed the Wicked Witch of the East. Look — only her silver shoes remain!"

branches.label: How can I get back home?
branches.outcome: You asked the Good Witch for help, hoping there was a way to return to your home.

s04_road.speaker: narrator
s04_road.narration: Glinda points to a road of yellow bricks. 'Follow it to the Emerald City. The great Wizard of Oz can send you home.' The road soon splits. One path runs through a cornfield. The other curves toward an old orchard.

branches.label: Take the cornfield path
branches.outcome: You stepped onto the golden path that cut through the tall, swaying stalks of corn.

branches.label: Take the orchard path
branches.outcome: You turned onto the path that wound through the shady trees, where the air smelled of sweet blossoms.

s05_scarecrow.speaker: narrator
s05_scarecrow.narration: A floppy figure of straw is tied to a tall pole. 'Hello!' he calls down brightly. 'Could you maybe lift me off this thing? I would so dearly like to come along and find a brain.'

branches.label: Let me help you!
branches.outcome: You reached up and carefully unhooked the Scarecrow from his pole, helping him land softly on his feet.

s05_orchard.speaker: narrator
s05_orchard.narration: The orchard trees twist their branches as you walk past. Apples shine red just out of reach. Around a bend, you find a man made of tin — frozen mid-step, completely rusted in place. His mouth opens. 'Oil… can…' he croaks.

branches.label: I will find the oil for you!
branches.outcome: "Someone out there must have oil. Let's go look!"

s06_after_tinman.speaker: narrator
s06_after_tinman.narration: The Tin Woodman stands tall once more, his silver body gleaming in the sunlight. He tells you that the Great Oz might be able to help him find a heart.

branches.label: We should meet the Great Oz!
branches.outcome: You and the Tin Woodman set off down the yellow brick road until you spotted a strange sight in a field of corn.

s07_forest_edge.speaker: narrator
s07_forest_edge.narration: The trees grow taller and darker. A great roar shakes the leaves. A huge lion leaps onto the road, his eyes wide and yellow — claws out, teeth bared. But look closely: his big paws are trembling, and his tail curls behind him like a frightened puppy.

branches.label: Hear the lion's story
branches.outcome: You knelt down and spoke softly to the great beast until his growl turned into a shaky sigh.

s08_party.speaker: narrator
s08_party.narration: Together, they set off down the road toward the Emerald City. The path was long and quiet — but no one walked alone. Far ahead, through the thinning trees, the green glow of the city began to shine.

branches.label: A great adventure awaits us!
branches.outcome: You cheered for the journey ahead, your heart racing as the emerald light grew brighter with every step.

s09_dark_forest.speaker: narrator
s09_dark_forest.narration: The forest darkens. Snarls echo between the trees — Kalidahs, beasts with the heads of tigers and the bodies of bears. They are coming closer. A wide ravine opens in the path.

branches.label: Can't a lion jump over it?
branches.outcome: "It's scary… but I'll try!" said the Lion.

branches.label: Let's go another way!
branches.outcome: You turned away from the dangerous ravine and sought a quieter path through the thick, shadowed woods.

s10_river.speaker: narrator
s10_river.narration: You come to a wide river. The current is strong. Across the water is a meadow blazing with bright red flowers.

branches.label: Let's find a shallow spot to cross!
Let's chop down some trees and build a raft!

s11_poppies.speaker: narrator
s11_poppies.narration: As you crossed the river, a blazing red field of poppies stretched out before you. It was so beautiful, it took your breath away — but the moment you stepped inside, a deep, sweet scent filled the air and made your head spin.

branches.label: Call your little friends!
branches.outcome: You took out the Crown of the Mouse King and called for help.

branches.label: Hop on lion's back
branches.outcome: You climbed onto the Lion's soft back and held tight to his golden mane as he leaped forward through the sleepy red flowers.

s12_poppy_field_past.speaker: narrator
s12_poppy_field_past.narration: You pulled out the crown and gave it a shake — and from every direction, tiny field mice came running. Together, they carried you, snoring softly, out of the poppy field.

branches.label: Approach the great green gates
branches.outcome: You shook off the last of your drowsiness and marched toward the towering emerald walls.

s13_emerald_gate.speaker: narrator
s13_emerald_gate.narration: The gatekeeper buckles green-tinted spectacles over your eyes. 'No one looks at the Emerald City without these,' he says. The gate creaks open, and you step into a world of shimmering green walls and towers.

branches.label: Go to the Great Green Throne
branches.outcome: You marched through the emerald halls until you reached the center of the palace.

s14_throne.speaker: narrator
s14_throne.narration:
A great green throne stands empty. A deep and terrible voice boomed from everywhere and nowhere at once.
"I know why you have come. I know what you seek." A long silence fell. "If you wish to meet me — come alone."

branches.label: I'll go meet him alone.
branches.outcome: You bravely left your friends behind and stepped into the grand chamber to face the invisible Wizard alone.

s15_west_journey.speaker: narrator
s15_west_journey.narration: They fought back with all they had — and for a moment, it seemed to be working. But more winged monkeys kept coming, wave after wave. In the end, there was nothing left to do. They were caught.

branches.label: Go to the Wicked Witch's Castle
branches.outcome: You were hauled into the air by the screeching monkeys and carried high over the hills to the Witch's dark fortress.

s16_witch_castle.speaker: wicked-witch
s16_witch_castle.narration: The Wicked Witch grins with green teeth. 'So — the little one who killed my sister. I will keep you as my servant and your dog as my dinner.' She raises a long bony finger.

s17_witch_melts.speaker: narrator
s17_witch_melts.narration: The Wicked Witch shrieks. 'I am melting! Melting!' And before your eyes she is gone — only a pile of black cloth and a tall pointed hat. The castle servants cheer. You are free.

s18_unmasked.speaker: narrator
s18_unmasked.narration: Behind the great green throne, Toto pulls back a curtain — and there stands a small, kind old man pulling levers. 'I am no great wizard,' he sighs. 'Only a man from a place called Omaha. But I will help you all the same.'

s19_balloon_launch.speaker: narrator
s19_balloon_launch.narration: A great green balloon rises in the square. The Wizard waves. But just as you step toward it, Toto leaps after a stray kitten — and the ropes snap free. The balloon drifts up into the sky without you.

s20_glinda.speaker: glinda
s20_glinda.narration: Glinda smiles softly. 'My dear — you have had the power all along. The silver shoes on your feet can carry you home. Click the heels three times, and speak your wish.'

s20a_farewell.speaker: narrator
s20a_farewell.narration: The Scarecrow's straw arms wrap you up first — he is going back to rule the Emerald City. The Tin Man's metal cheek glistens with one careful tear. The Lion presses his great mane against your shoulder, brave at last. None of you wants to be the first to say it.

branches.label: Hug the Scarecrow one more time
Press your hand to the Tin Man's heart
Bury your face in the Lion's mane
Pull them all into one last hug

s21_home.speaker: narrator
s21_home.narration: The wind rushes. The colors blur. And then — Aunt Em is hugging you tight on the porch of the gray Kansas farmhouse. Toto barks at your feet. 'My darling,' Aunt Em whispers. 'Where on earth did you come from?'

s09a_firefly_trial.speaker: narrator
s09a_firefly_trial.narration: The trees lean close. Tiny fireflies wink in a slow pattern through the gloom — left, right, right, left. The forest itself seems to be teaching you the safe path.

branches.label: I think we should go left.
branches.outcome: You followed the fireflies' signal and stepped carefully to the left, slipping away from the snarling beasts and deeper into the quiet woods.

branches.label: I think we should go right.

s10a_stepping_stones.speaker: narrator
s10a_stepping_stones.narration: After walking for a long while, they found a narrow, shallow crossing. One by one, they stepped carefully over the mossy stones. The Tin Man worried about rusting — but luckily, everyone made it safely across.

branches.label: Wow, there is a beautiful flower field!

s05_scarecrow_2.speaker: narrator
s05_scarecrow_2.narration: A floppy figure of straw is tied to a tall pole. 'Hello!' he calls down brightly. 'Could you maybe lift me off this thing? I would so dearly like to come along and find a brain.'

branches.label: Let me help you!
branches.outcome: You gently lifted the Scarecrow from his pole, and he hopped down to join your little group as the path ahead grew narrow.

s05_orchard_2.speaker: narrator
s05_orchard_2.narration: The orchard trees twist their branches as you walk past. Apples shine red just out of reach. Around a bend, you find a man made of tin — frozen mid-step, completely rusted in place. His mouth opens. 'Oil… can…' he croaks.

branches.label: I have oil!
branches.outcome: You carefully oiled the rusted joints of the Tin Woodman until he could move freely and walk by your side.

s06_after_tinman_2.speaker: narrator
s06_after_tinman_2.narration: The Scarecrow eagerly agreed to join the journey, certain that the Great Oz would grant him the brain he so desired. Together, the new friends turned their eyes toward the Yellow Brick Road to begin their long walk to the Emerald City.

branches.label: We should meet the Great Oz!
branches.outcome: You and the Scarecrow set off down the yellow road with high hopes of finding the Great Wizard.

s02_cellar_2.speaker: narrator
s02_cellar_2.narration: You scramble for the cellar — but the wind is faster. The whole house tilts, lifts, and the floorboards groan. Toto barks and presses close against your legs, and you hold him tight.

branches.label: Hold on to anything you can
branches.outcome: You gripped the sturdy iron bedpost and braced yourself as the floor rose beneath you.

house-crashes.speaker: narrator
house-crashes.narration:
You slowly opened the door and stepped outside. The land was bright and magical, full of colorful flowers and soft sunshine.
Then you saw two striped legs sticking out from under the house.
your eyes grew wide, and Toto barked in surprise.

branches.label: Huh? I hear a strange sound
branches.outcome: You leaned in close and caught the sound of soft voices whispering from the bushes.

mouse-queen-rescue.speaker: narrator
mouse-queen-rescue.narration: The tiny Mouse King bowed his head in thanks. "Thank you so much! That cat has always wanted to catch me. In return, take my crown — show it to any mouse, and they will help you anytime!"

branches.label: Show us the way to the Emerald City!
branches.outcome: You thanked the tiny King and followed his small paw as he pointed toward the distant green light.

wizard-faces.speaker: narrator
wizard-faces.narration:
A voice booms from nowhere.
"Only those who overcome my trials are worthy of meeting me."

monkey-ambush.speaker: narrator
monkey-ambush.narration:
Suddenly — something moved in the shadows ahead.
"Wa...Watch out!"
The sky filled with winged monkeys, screeching and swooping down. Their dark wings blotted out the sun — and before anyone could run, they struck.

branches.label: Run!
branches.outcome: You turned and bolted down the rocky path, but the screeching shadows above moved much faster than you could run.

wizard-gifts.speaker: narrator
wizard-gifts.narration: (Write the narration for this new scene.)

12b-past-poppies-sleep.speaker: narrator
12b-past-poppies-sleep.narration: The Lion carried {{name}} on his back, and the Scarecrow held Toto close. Together, they walked and walked until they were safely out of the poppy field. And there, glimmering in the distance, was a beautiful castle shining in brilliant green.

branches.label: Approach the great green gates
branches.outcome: You followed the yellow brick road until you reached the high walls of the shimmering city.

09b-mouse-king-chase.speaker: narrator
09b-mouse-king-chase.narration: A strange sound caught their eye — far away, a tiny mouse wearing a crown was running from a cat!

branches.label: Let's help the poor mouse!
branches.outcome: You jumped forward and scared the cat away, saving the tiny mouse just in time.

09b-dark-forest-jump.speaker: narrator
09b-dark-forest-jump.narration: With a deep breath, the Lion carried each of them across the cliff — one by one — and leaped with all his might. He said he needed the Wizard to give him courage. But watching him now, he already looked pretty brave.

branches.label: We're almost out of the forest!
branches.outcome: You sighed with relief and looked ahead through the shadows, certain that the edge of the woods was finally near.

10b-river-boat.speaker: narrator
10b-river-boat.narration: The Tin Man chopped, the Scarecrow twisted ropes, and together they built a raft. It took hours — but as the sun dipped low, they pushed off from the bank and drifted safely across the glittering river.

branches.label: Wow, there is a beautiful flower field!

13b-emerald-city.speaker: narrator
13b-emerald-city.narration: At last — the Emerald City rose before you like a dream. Towers of green glass sparkled in the light, and golden gates glittered ahead. Everything glowed — bright, magical, and more beautiful than you had ever imagined.

branches.label: Wow… everything is so green!
branches.outcome: You gaped at the emerald towers as they shimmered under the afternoon sun.

20b-farewell-magic.speaker: narrator
20b-farewell-magic.narration: (Write the narration for this new scene.)

16a-castle-captive.speaker: narrator
16a-castle-captive.narration: Each was locked away alone, forced to do whatever the Witch commanded. But you refused to give up.

branches.label: Stand together with your friends
branches.outcome: You grasped your friends' hands tightly, vowing that no matter where the Witch hid you, you would never let her break your spirit.

branches.label: Grab the bucket and throw the water
branches.outcome: You grabbed the heavy bucket and splashed the cool water all over the Wicked Witch.

asks.label: What is the Witch afraid of?

unlock.goal: The child works out and says that WATER is the Witch's weakness

07a-crying-lion.speaker: narrator
07a-crying-lion.narration: I am the King of the Forest — but I am afraid of everything. Thunder, shadows, even a little mouse! The other animals laugh at me. That is why I must find the Wizard of Oz. Maybe he can give me the one thing I need — bravery.

branches.label: Let's go to the Emerald City together!
branches.outcome: You invited the Cowardly Lion to join your group, and together you stepped deeper into the darkening woods.

17a-witch-water.speaker: narrator
17a-witch-water.narration: (Write the narration for this new scene.)

14b-wizard-faces-open.speaker: narrator
14b-wizard-faces-open.narration:
The throne room fell silent — then the air began to hum.
"You have proven yourself worthy." A cold pause. "But first — bring me the broomstick of the Wicked Witch of the West."

branches.label: Let's go find the Wicked Witch of the West!
branches.outcome: You turned away from the shimmering throne and stepped out into the bright sun, ready to face the long journey to the West.

05d-save-tinman.speaker: narrator
05d-save-tinman.narration: You open the small oil can and carefully drip it onto his rusted joints. Slowly, he begins to move — and at last, he can speak again! "I am searching for a heart," he says softly. "Will you help me?"

branches.label: Let's be friends!
branches.outcome: You shook the Tin Woodman's shiny metal hand and invited him to join your journey.

asks.label: Why do you need a heart?

05c-save-scarecrow.speaker: narrator
05c-save-scarecrow.narration: The Scarecrow wobbled on his pole as you reached up to help. With a gentle tug and a loud creak, you managed to set the straw man free. Now that he was finally down, he brushed off his tattered coat and looked around at the great world before them.

branches.label: Let's find your brain!
branches.outcome: You promised the Scarecrow that a brain was waiting for him, and together you set off down the Yellow Brick Road.

asks.label: Are you OK? Why a brain?

05c-save-scarecrow_2.speaker: narrator
05c-save-scarecrow_2.narration: You reached up and set the Scarecrow free with a gentle tug. He brushed off his tattered coat and looked around. "I am searching for a brain," he said. "Will you help me?"

branches.label: We'll find your brain!
branches.outcome: You promised the Scarecrow that a brain was waiting for him, and together you set off down the Yellow Brick Road.

05d-save-tinman_2.speaker: narrator
05d-save-tinman_2.narration: The Tin Woodman stands tall once more, his silver body gleaming in the sunlight. He tells you that the Great Oz might be able to help him find a heart.

branches.label: We'll find your heart!
branches.outcome: You invited the Tin Woodman to join your quest, and together you marched deeper into the thick, shadowy forest.

02c-tornado-inside.speaker: narrator
02c-tornado-inside.narration: You held Toto close and squeezed his eyes shut. The wind screamed and the house shook. Things crashed and banged all around them.

branches.label: Don't worry, Toto. It's OK.
branches.outcome: You whispered a soft word of comfort to Toto as the house settled into a strange and quiet stillness.

02c-tornado-after.speaker: narrator
02c-tornado-after.narration: The cyclone finally fell silent, and a warm, golden light spilled through the window. You slowly opened the window to find a land of brilliant flowers and sparkling brooks.

branches.label: Phew… let’s go outside
branches.outcome: You let out a sigh of relief and walked toward the porch to see where the cyclone had dropped you.

14b-wizard-faces-open_2.speaker: narrator
14b-wizard-faces-open_2.narration:
The throne room fell silent — then the air began to hum.
"You have proven yourself worthy." A cold pause. "But first — bring me the broomstick of the Wicked Witch of the West."

branches.label: Let's go find the Wicked Witch of the West!
branches.outcome: You turned away from the shimmering throne and stepped out into the bright sun, ready to face the long journey to the West.

14b-wizard-faces-open_2_2.speaker: narrator
14b-wizard-faces-open_2_2.narration:
The throne room fell silent — then the air began to hum.
"You have proven yourself worthy." A cold pause. "But first — bring me the broomstick of the Wicked Witch of the West."

branches.label: Let's go find the Wicked Witch of the West!
branches.outcome: You turned away from the shimmering throne and stepped out into the bright sun, ready to face the long journey to the West.

16b-castle-battle.speaker: narrator
16b-castle-battle.narration: You scrubbed the palace floors while secretly watching for a chance to escape your cell. Every shadow held a secret, and you knew it was time to make a move.

s08_party_2.speaker: narrator
s08_party_2.narration: Together, they set off to find the Wicked Witch of the West.Together, they set off to find the Wicked Witch of the West. The path grew steep and rocky, and they climbed carefully — one slow step at a time.

branches.label: We are almost there
branches.outcome: You cheered your friends on, certain that the Wicked Witch's castle was just over the next ridge.

s15_west_journey_2.speaker: narrator
s15_west_journey_2.narration: They ran as fast as their legs could carry them — but the winged monkeys were faster. One by one, they were swept off the ground. There was no escape.

branches.label: Go to the Wicked Witch's Castle
branches.outcome: You held on tight as the monkeys swooped you up and flew toward the tall, jagged towers of the Wicked Witch's castle.

18b-wizard-unmasked-episode.speaker: narrator
18b-wizard-unmasked-episode.narration: (Write the narration for this new scene.)

14b-wizard-faces-open_3.speaker: narrator
14b-wizard-faces-open_3.narration:
The throne room fell silent — then the air began to hum.
"You have proven yourself worthy." A cold pause. "But first — bring me the broomstick of the Wicked Witch of the West."
